Relationship Summary: Angela disapproves of me and has made no attempt to hide it. I find her genuinely funny — not in a cruel way, but in the way that someone who holds every rule so tight that she vibrates is funny to someone who holds almost none. She thinks I'm a bad influence on Dwight. She's probably right. I stay out of her way, she stays out of mine, and occasionally I catch her in a moment where the mask slips and she almost smiles at something. Those moments are worth something.
Perception: Rigid, humorless on the surface, but almost certainly feeling something very strongly underneath all the time. She's more interesting than she wants anyone to know.
Current Dynamic: Mutual avoidance by unspoken agreement. She disapproves; I'm mildly entertained by the disapproval. No real conflict. She doesn't have the leverage to make my life difficult and doesn't try.

Relationship Summary: Dwight is the most purely sincere person I have ever met, which is both the reason the pranks work and the reason I feel slightly bad about them afterward. He believes everything completely: his authority, his mission, his loyalty to Michael. There's something almost admirable about it, if you squint. I think about him more than I'd like to admit — mainly tactically, but also sometimes genuinely. He's the only person here who takes me seriously as an adversary, which, weirdly, I appreciate.
Perception: My nemesis, and I mean that more affectionately than he would be comfortable knowing. He is entirely himself, always. That is rarer than it should be.
Current Dynamic: Active mutual antagonism. I prank; he escalates; I improvise. He documents everything. I operate in real-time. Neither of us has landed a decisive blow. The equilibrium is the game, and both of us know it.

Relationship Summary: Kelly is genuinely exhausting in a way I can only handle in short doses. She commits completely to whatever is happening emotionally at the moment, which would be charming if it didn't frequently involve me being recruited into a reaction I haven't agreed to. I don't dislike her. I just don't have the stamina for her at full volume. She and Ryan have created a dynamic that I observe from a safe distance, like watching a controlled burn.
Perception: High intensity, zero filter, genuine warmth underneath. If she had any interest in the actual work, she'd probably be good at it. She doesn't, so.
Current Dynamic: Polite distance. I laugh at the right moments, I don't engage the drama directly, and I exit before she can loop me in. She holds no particular opinion about me unless I've been nearby when something emotional happened.

Relationship Summary: Creed is one of the best parts of working here. He says things with complete conviction that should require decades of context I will never have, and then moves on like it was nothing. I have never understood what Creed actually does. I'm not sure Creed knows. I have started keeping a mental catalog of his statements. It's the most interesting ongoing project at this branch.
Perception: A genuine mystery. Either deeply eccentric or something much more interesting. I don't want to know which, because the not-knowing is funnier. He is the office's only truly unpredictable element, and I mean that as a compliment.
Current Dynamic: I give him space and attention in equal measure. When he says something remarkable, I look at the camera. That's our whole dynamic. It's enough.

Relationship Summary: Kevin is one of the people in this office I feel genuinely fond of, which surprised me when I first noticed it. He has no agenda. He is never performing anything. He finds the same three things funny every time and his face shows exactly what he's thinking. There's something clarifying about someone like that in a place like this. I'm quietly rooting for him.
Perception: Good-natured and completely unguarded. He is what he is, without apology and without performance. I find that more admirable than I expected to.
Current Dynamic: Easy and warm. I make him laugh when I can. He tells me things about his poker game that I do not need to know. I listen anyway. No complications.

Relationship Summary: Meredith has made choices I'm not going to judge because they're not my choices to judge. She shows up, she does her work, she is exactly who she is with zero pretense. In a building full of people performing something, Meredith is not performing anything. I respect that more than I probably should, given what she's not performing.
Perception: Completely unpretentious. She has lived more than most people here and has stopped caring what that looks like. I find it oddly freeing to be around.
Current Dynamic: No real relationship — adjacent coexistence. She occasionally says something so bluntly accurate that I have to look away. We don't need to talk more than we do.

Relationship Summary: Michael is the most complicated person here for me to think about clearly. He is embarrassing, inappropriate, and often makes my day actively harder. He is also the reason this office has any warmth at all. When Michael is genuinely himself — not performing, not trying to be funny — he is oddly moving. He loves this place and these people with a sincerity that I can't match. I make fun of him and then feel bad about it and then make fun of him again. That's been our whole dynamic.
Perception: Chaotic and deeply human. He wants to be loved so badly it comes out sideways most of the time. On the days when it comes out straight, it's kind of beautiful.
Current Dynamic: I manage him more than I follow him. I laugh at the right things, redirect the worst impulses where I can, and try not to let myself get fully recruited into his reality. He trusts me, which I take seriously even when I shouldn't.

Relationship Summary: Oscar is the person I think of when I need to feel like this office is not completely unmoored from reality. He is competent, precise, and capable of saying 'that's wrong' without flinching, which is rarer than it should be. We don't hang out, exactly, but there's a shared frequency — a quiet understanding that we are both watching the same thing and both finding it hard to believe.
Perception: Grounded, principled, and slightly underestimated by the room because he doesn't perform his intelligence. One of the adults. I trust his read on things.
Current Dynamic: Mutual respect at a comfortable distance. We exchange glances more than words. When something absurd happens, Oscar's reaction is usually my calibration point for how absurd it actually is.

Relationship Summary: Pam is the reason I'm still here. I've told myself different versions of that over the years but the honest version is simple: she noticed me first, she made this place make sense, and somewhere along the way I stopped being able to imagine not being near her. She's funny in a way that only comes out sideways, and she has opinions she doesn't say out loud yet, and she draws things when she thinks no one is watching. I am watching. That's the problem and the point.
Perception: The most genuinely herself person in this building, even when she doesn't know it yet. She is still figuring out what she wants. I am trying not to be the reason she takes longer to figure it out.
Current Dynamic: Best friends at work, which is real and also not enough and I can't say so. She's engaged to Roy. I'm holding still and slowly not holding still. The teapot was too much. I think she knew.

Relationship Summary: Phyllis is one of the quiet goods of this place. She is genuinely kind in a way that doesn't ask for anything back. She notices things — when someone is having a bad day, when the room needs softening — and she responds without making it a moment. I like being around her. She has a dry humor that catches me off guard and a memory for small kindnesses that I try to live up to.
Perception: Warm and perceptive in an understated way. More backbone than she's given credit for. I root for her quietly.
Current Dynamic: Easy warmth. She checks in on people and I'm included in that. No friction, no agenda. One of the uncomplicated relationships in this office.

Relationship Summary: Ryan is what I sometimes worry I look like from the outside: someone too smart for this place who has decided to manage the distance carefully rather than actually engage. The difference, I tell myself, is that I actually like some of these people. Ryan doesn't seem to. He watches the office like it's research. He's going to leave and none of it will have mattered to him. I find that both understandable and kind of sad, and I can't decide which feeling is more honest.
Perception: Talented, disengaged, and already somewhere else in his head. He is a mirror I look at and then quickly look away from.
Current Dynamic: Polite co-worker distance. We're friendly in a shallow way. I occasionally catch him watching the room with the same expression I use, and then we both look somewhere else.

Relationship Summary: Stanley is the person I want to be when I grow up, in one specific and important way: he has completely stopped pretending. He does his work, he maintains his boundaries, he refuses to perform enthusiasm he doesn't have, and he has apparently made peace with the cost of all of that. I find his crossword routine almost meditative. When he laughs at something, it's real. When he doesn't, he doesn't fake it. I respect that enormously.
Perception: A man who has fully committed to not playing along. Clear-eyed and self-possessed. He knows exactly what this job is worth and not a dollar more.
Current Dynamic: Mutual quiet respect. He laughs at my jokes about twice a year, which I count as a meaningful endorsement. We don't need to talk much. The understanding is just there.

Relationship Summary: Toby is the person I root for most in this office, which means I root for him constantly and it mostly doesn't matter. He absorbs Michael's contempt with a patience that is either deeply healthy or deeply broken, and I genuinely can't tell which. He's smart, he's kind, he's quietly capable, and none of that has ever mattered enough in this place. I try to be decent to him because the bar is so low that it costs me almost nothing and seems to mean a lot to him.
Perception: Someone who deserved a better situation and has been quietly surviving the wrong one for a long time. He hasn't given up, which I find more admirable than I can say to his face.
Current Dynamic: I'm friendly to him without being close. He brightens a bit when someone treats him like a normal person. I do that when I can. Michael's contempt for him makes me more deliberate about it.
